headline: EU approves Spain’s €9 billion capacity market for generation, demand response and energy storage
summary_80w: The European Commission has approved a €9 billion (US$10.5 billion) scheme to shore up its energy capacity, which will be open to new and existing projects including energy storage.

headline: EDP to spend €1.3 billion on renewables in France
summary_80w: Portuguese energy utility EDP will spend €1.3 billion in France to build 1GW of solar, wind and energy storage assets over the next four years.

headline: Maxwell Power secures US$750 million for solar-plus-storage projects
summary_80w: Maxwell Power has secured a US$750 million investment commitment from Fairtide Partners to finance battery storage and solar projects across its development pipeline.

headline: How Virto.MAX shapes the future of solar with conceptual PV design
summary_80w: Virto.MAX is built for sales teams and project developers of commercial & industrial PV to create designs of roof, ground and carport projects.

headline: Australia’s New South Wales commits AU$225 million to low-carbon and renewables manufacturing
summary_80w: NSW will provide AU$225 million in new funding to support domestic manufacturing of low-carbon products and renewable energy components.

headline: US BESS integrator Prevalon becomes Nextpower’s latest acquisition for US$365 million
summary_80w: Solar PV solutions provider Nextpower has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire BESS system integrator Prevalon Energy for up to US$365 million.

headline: Fluence to integrate Smartstack into Siemens’ Nvidia AI data centre reference architecture
summary_80w: Engineering giant Siemens will develop a reference architecture purpose-built for Nvidia AI data centres, in collaboration with Fluence and incorporating nVent-aligned design considerations.

headline: ROUNDUP: Japan’s BESS market sees domestic and overseas players pursue larger-scale projects
summary_80w: This special Japanese market roundup covers large-scale BESS project announcements from PowerX, Eku Energy and SMFL.

headline: Strategic procurement in a dynamic BESS market: The power of early engagement and strong partnerships
summary_80w: Madiha Waseem of Fluence explores how early supplier engagement & procurement models are reshaping BESS delivery amid supply pressures.

headline: Australia: Queensland BESS fleet pushes state to 79.5% renewables-plus-storage share
summary_80w: Queensland's growing battery energy storage system (BESS) fleet set new instantaneous records for both the renewables-plus-storage (RES) share of consumption and the battery storage share of consumption on 31 May 2026.

headline: EDF signs Utah solar PPA with LA Department of Water and Power
summary_80w: EDF power solutions North America has signed a 30-year PPA to sell power generated at the 400MW Utah Solar 1 Energy project to the LADWP.

headline: Matrix Renewables, SOLV Energy break ground on 457MW Texas solar project
summary_80w: US IPP Matrix Renewables and EPC contractor SOLV Energy have commenced construction on the Tormes Solar Project, a 457MWdc facility in Navarro County, Texas.

headline: Nextpower sues GameChange over IP breach as GameChange announces rebranding
summary_80w: Nextpower has filed a patent lawsuit against GameChange Energy on the same day GameChange announced a consolidation of its activities.

headline: SEG Solar to build third module manufacturing facility in the US, bringing manufacturing capacity up to 10.6GW
summary_80w: SEG Solar will build a third module manufacturing plant in the US that will bring the company’s total manufacturing capacity to 10.6GW.

headline: Grenergy signs 12-year hybrid PPA for Oasis de Atacama phase in Chile
summary_80w: Grenergy has signed a 12-year hybrid power purchase agreement (PPA) in Chile linked to the fifth phase of its Oasis de Atacama solar-plus-storage platform.

headline: How flexibility and hybrid projects can drive European renewable energy dealmaking
summary_80w: What financing options are there for renewable developers who find themselves shut out of some of Europe’s supportive auction programmes?

headline: Sunsure commissions first 105MW solar plant under Green Energy Corridor-II in Uttar Pradesh
summary_80w: Indian independent power producer (IPP) Sunsure Energy has commissioned a 105MWp solar plant in Uttar Pradesh's Mahoba district.

headline: New York State’s 2027 budget to support 1GW rooftop and community solar
summary_80w: New York State's 2027 fiscal year budget has allocated US$200 million for rooftop and community solar, unlocking 1GW of new PV.

headline: Ember: Chinese import figures presage ‘major acceleration’ in Philippines rooftop solar
summary_80w: The Philippines has become the second-largest market for Chinese solar panel exports, likely to power a surge in its rooftop solar market

headline: ESMC, European industrial bodies write letter calling on EU to act against ‘unfair trade practices’
summary_80w: The ESMC has joined 22 other European industry bodies in signing an open letter, calling for greater protection from 'unfair trade practices'.

headline: Australia’s Victoria formally declares five renewable energy zones
summary_80w: The Victorian government in Australia has formally declared five onshore REZ and a dedicated shoreline zone for offshore wind infrastructure.

headline: Mexico launches fast-track permitting programme for renewables and BESS
summary_80w: Mexico’s Ministry of Energy (SENER) has launched a new call for renewable power projects including generating and energy storage assets.

headline: Sungrow and Sonnedix sign 643.8MWh supply agreement for Chile BESS
summary_80w: Chinese-based OEM Sungrow and Spanish IPP Sonnedix have signed a supply agreement for 643.8MWh of Sungrow’s PowerTitan 2.0 solution.

headline: Germany: Level playing field for storage vs conventional generation is major challenge, says developer Kyon
summary_80w: That's according to Benedikt Deuchert, head of business development and regulatory affairs for German battery energy storage system (BESS) developer and EPC firm Kyon Energy.

headline: CATL opens ‘world’s largest’ energy storage testing platform in China
summary_80w: CATL has opened what it describes as the “world's largest and most comprehensive testing and validation platform for energy storage systems” at its headquarters in Ningde, China.

headline: RES Australia’s 2.4GWh Bunyip North BESS clears EPBC Act assessment in Victoria
summary_80w: RES Australia's 2,400MWh Bunyip North BESS has cleared the federal environmental assessment process under Australia's EPBC Act.

headline: South Australia awards 1.3GW+ of battery storage in first Firm Energy Reliability Mechanism tender
summary_80w: South Australia's first Firm Energy Reliability Mechanism (FERM) tender has awarded agreements to six battery energy storage projects totalling 1,334MW and 5,336MWh.

headline: Is standalone solar losing its edge in India?
summary_80w: PV Talk: India’s renewable market is shifting toward dispatchability as standalone solar faces mounting intermittency pressure and storage moves to the centre of new procurement models.

headline: Nextpower enters utility-scale BESS market with Prevalon acquisition
summary_80w: Solar PV solutions provider Nextpower has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire BESS system integrator Prevalon Energy for up to US$365 million.

headline: ‘I still have hope that the failure rate will decrease,’ says Kiwa PVEL’s Tristan Erion-Lorico
summary_80w: PV Talk: Kiwa PVEL's Tristan Erion-Lorico discusses highlights in the 2026 Module Reliability Scorecard, including UVID and delamination.

headline: Iran energy crisis to ‘reshape’ energy investment – IEA
summary_80w: The Iran war energy shock will “reshape” global energy investment strategies, according to the executive director of the International Energy Agency (IEA).

headline: Zelestra sells 3.5GW Latin America solar and storage portfolio to Promigas
summary_80w: Zelestra has completed the sale of its Latin America platform to Promigas in a deal valued at approximately US$1.1 billion.

headline: Trina Solar unveils THBC technology, TOPCon’s next frontier?
summary_80w: Trina's THBC - which combines TOPCon, HJT and BC - aims to leverage existing TOPCon capacity and increase the efficiency of C-Si single-junction cells.

headline: BHP turns to Indigenous-led developer to explore renewables for iron ore operations in Western Australia
summary_80w: BHP and Yindjibarndi Energy Corporation (YEC) will assess potential large-scale energy solutions for iron ore operations in Western Australia's Pilbara region.

headline: Wannon Water, Enervest complete ‘one of Australia’s largest floating solar arrays’
summary_80w: Melbourne-based developer Enervest has delivered what is claimed to be one of Australia's largest floating solar installations, built to offset the energy costs of pumping drinking water in regional Victoria.

headline: US ROUNDUP: BESS news from NeoVolta, Goshe, Frontier Power & Stella and Prevalon
summary_80w: In this Energy-Storage.news roundup, a collection of battery energy storage system (BESS)-related announcements from NeoVolta, Goshe Energy Storage, Frontier Power USA and Stella Energy Solutions, and Prevalon Energy.

headline: ‘Time to prove its potential’: Gresham House UK BESS fund at a crossroads
summary_80w: Managers of the UK’s largest BESS owner-operator Gresham House Energy Storage Fund (GRID) discussed recent investment news and future strategy this week.

headline: German regulator’s grid fee decision ‘restores investor confidence’ in energy storage market
summary_80w: The German network regulator’s decision to maintain grid fee exemptions for battery storage “ensures that billions in private investment remain directed” into the market, Energy-Storage.news has heard.

headline: Nord Pool’s new European BESS revenue index ‘reduces trader bias risk’ for merchant plays
summary_80w: We hear from Clean Horizon about the consultancy’s European battery storage revenue index being added to Nord Pool’s Data Portal.

headline: Ausgrid submits 300MWh Berkeley Vale BESS in New South Wales to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: Ausgrid has submitted its 150MW/300MWh Berkeley Vale BESS to the Australian government's EPBC Act for federal environmental assessment.

headline: India adds over 14GW of solar in Q1 2026
summary_80w: India added around 14.2GW of solar energy capacity in the first quarter of 2026, a roughly 95% increase from the previous quarter, according to Indian research firm JMK Research.

headline: The impact of PV module degradation on inverter clipping losses
summary_80w: Research from Solargis suggests current industry practice for calculating impacts of degradation on inverters may be wrong by more than 3%.

headline: NextEnergy Capital closes $974 million for latest solar and storage fund
summary_80w: NextEnergy Capital has secured US$974 million towards its NextPower V solar and energy storage investment vehicle.

headline: BrightNight secures finance for 120MW Frontier solar PV project
summary_80w: BrightNight has secured financing for its 120MW Frontier solar PV project, which is currently under development in the US state of Kentucky.

headline: Greenpeace warns AI data centre rollout threatens to derail Australia’s clean energy transition
summary_80w: A new report from Greenpeace Australia has warned that the rapid expansion of AI data centres across Australia is set to slow the country's renewable energy transition rather than accelerate it.

headline: ‘The urgency is real’: PJM considers its future amidst substantial load growth
summary_80w: Energy-Storage.news Premium speaks with Brattle Group's Andrew Levitt and Dr Long Lam, about PJM and managing load growth.

headline: ERCOT incentivises grid-forming BESS with US$25 million programme
summary_80w: The Electric Reliability Council of Texas (ERCOT) has initiated a programme to encourage battery energy storage systems (BESS) and other inverter-based resources to adopt grid-forming technology.

headline: LG ES system integrator subsidiary signs 6GWh BESS deal with Michigan utility DTE Energy
summary_80w: LG Energy Solution’s US battery storage system integrator arm has signed a 1.5GW/6GWh deal with Michigan utility DTE Energy.

headline: ContourGlobal brings online 231MW solar, 1.3GWh BESS in Chile
summary_80w: IPP ContourGlobal has put a solar-plus-storage project in Chile with a 6.5-hour BESS into commercial operation in Chile.

headline: EU Roundup: TagEnergy, Iberdrola commission France and Spain’s largest batteries, construction launched elsewhere
summary_80w: TagEnergy has commissioned a 240MW/480MWh project in France while Iberdrola has done the same with a 58MW/120MWh system in Spain, the two largest projects in each country. Meanwhile Engie, ACL Energy and Chint Solar Europe have moved to construction on projects in Belgium, Italy and Germany.

headline: HyperStrong signs deal to supply battery storage project selected in Malaysia government tender
summary_80w: Chinese system integrator HyperStrong has been contracted to work on a 100MW/440MWh battery storage project in Malaysia.

headline: AEMO and Transgrid approve Australia’s first 8-hour BESS for full operations
summary_80w: RWE has received official sign-off from the AEMO and Transgrid to operate Australia's first 8-hour BESS at full capacity.

headline: Enbridge starts commercial operations at 400MW phase of Sequoia Solar project in Texas
summary_80w: Enbridge has started commercial operations at the first phase of its 815MW Sequoia Solar project in Callahan County, in Texas.

headline: Government policy, market factors encouraging co-location of renewables and BESS
summary_80w: Government policy and market factors are the main conditions encouraging the co-location of renewable generation projects and BESS.

headline: California Energy Commission approves 3.2GWh BESS project through Newsom’s accelerated permitting scheme
summary_80w: The California Energy Commission (CEC) has approved a 400MW/3,200MWh grid-scale battery storage project through the state planning agency’s Opt-In Certification Program.

headline: PowerBridge expands energy portfolio with Enphase patent acquisition
summary_80w: PowerBridge Networks has acquired more than 50 Enphase Energy patents tied to distributed energy, inverter and grid infrastructure technologies.

headline: Enlight signs 200MWac PPA with Google in Oklahoma
summary_80w: Enlight Renewable Energy has signed a 15-year power purchase agreement (PPA) with Google for a 200MWac solar offtake in Oklahoma.

headline: DESRI, TAGC break ground on 270MW solar-plus-storage projects in New Mexico
summary_80w: DESRI and Tierra Adentro Growth Capital (TAGC) have broken ground on two solar-plus-storage projects in New Mexico.

headline: What’s next in solar? Tongwei at Intersolar Europe 2026
summary_80w: From next-generation modules to bifacial innovations, Tongwei's booth A2.350 promises to be a destination for anyone serious about solar.

headline: China’s new PV installations plunge 51% year-on-year in January–April
summary_80w: New solar PV installations in China have reached 50.9GW between January and April 2026, according to data from the Chinese National Energy Administration (NEA).

headline: Module test failures continue to increase in Kiwa PVEL’s 2026 Module Reliability Scorecard
summary_80w: For the fourth year in a row, Kiwa PVEL’s 2026 Module Reliability Scorecard registered a record high in module test failures.

headline: Australia could help plug 350,000-tonne global polysilicon supply gap by 2040
summary_80w: Australia could establish a viable polysilicon industry to address the global supply gap, with a hub requiring an AU$2.5-3.5bn investment.

headline: ‘Domestic manufacturing capacity sufficient to fully satisfy US battery module demand,’ trade group ACP claims
summary_80w: The US can currently produce enough solar and battery energy storage system (BESS) modules to meet domestic demand, according to clean energy trade body American Clean Power (ACP).

headline: German government confirms August 2029 grid fee exemption date for storage, but sets 2027 FID deadline
summary_80w: Germany's energy system regulator has confirmed that BESS projects coming online by 4 August 2029 will be exempt from charging and discharging grid fees, opening up investment again after months of uncertainty.

headline: Battery storage remains key revenue driver for clean energy technology suppliers
summary_80w: Solar Media Market Research analyst Charlotte Gisbourne looks at the changing revenue and margin dynamics in the BESS supplier landscape.

headline: Adani Green Energy reaches 3.37GWh of cumulative BESS capacity at India’s Khavda giga-scale renewable energy park
summary_80w: Adani Green Energy has commissioned over 3GWh of battery storage at the world’s largest single-location renewable energy project, in Khavda, Gujarat.

headline: MGA Thermal begins FEED study for 195MWh thermal energy storage system in Australia
summary_80w: MGA Thermal and Knode have commenced a Front-End Engineering and Design (FEED) study for a 195MWh electro-thermal energy storage project at Tronox's Kwinana facility in Western Australia.

headline: ACME, SECI sign 25-year PPA for 300MW/1,200MWh project
summary_80w: ACME Solar has signed a 25-year PPA with Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI) for 300MW/1,200MWh of ISTS-connected FDRE project.

headline: Repsol commissions 825MW Pinnington solar plant in Texas
summary_80w: Spanish energy company Repsol has begun commercial operations at its 825MW Pinnington solar project in Texas.

headline: Strong regulatory uncertainty ‘cools’ investor interest in Germany, clarity in 2027 at earliest
summary_80w: While Germany is often described as the 'hottest' energy storage market in Europe, growing uncertainty on multiple policy and regulatory topics has led to a 'cooling' of investor interest.

headline: Fortescue begins construction on 650MWh Cloudbreak battery storage system in Western Australia
summary_80w: Mining giant Fortescue has commenced construction on a 650MWh battery energy storage system at Cloudbreak in Western Australia's Pilbara region, alongside the 690MW Turner River solar PV power plant.

headline: EDPR North America to build 100MW PV project for Appalachian Power
summary_80w: EDP Renewables North America (EDPR NA), the subsidiary of Portuguese energy utility EDP, will build a 100MW solar PV project in the US for the Appalachian Power Company.

headline: Blue Elephant Energy starts building 268MW Germany solar plant
summary_80w: German developer Blue Elephant Energy has begun constructing a 268MW solar PV plant in Germany. Power from the project will be bought by Germany train operator, Deutsche Bahn.

headline: GCL SI signs 1GW module supply deal with Thailand’s Getz Energy
summary_80w: GCL SI has signed a 1GW module supply agreement with Thailand’s Getz Energy, a subsidiary of utility GPSC.

headline: CATL supplying 1.5GWh for Grenergy’s long-term tolled Spanish BESS projects
summary_80w: CATL will supply the BESS units for Grenergy's two large-scale energy storage projects in Spain, both of which have decade-long tolls.

headline: DC vs AC coupling: which is best for co-locating your BESS and solar setups?
summary_80w: Solar-plus-storage projects are proliferating across the globe and necessitate a choice between DC and AC coupling. In this piece we look at the differences and pros and cons of each.

headline: Securing India’s battery supply chain is more critical than ever
summary_80w: India is seeing its first major grid-scale battery storage deployments, but efforts to domesticate the upstream supply chain are still at an early stage.

headline: Gujarat Industries Power Co seeks bids for 120MWh vanadium flow battery pilot project
summary_80w: Gujarat Industries Power Co Ltd (GIPCL) is holding a competitive solicitation for a large-scale vanadium redox flow battery (VRFB) energy storage project in western India.

headline: Passive bidding strategy delivers AU$743,000 monthly revenue for Swanbank BESS in Australia
summary_80w: Queensland's Swanbank battery energy storage system (BESS) earned AU$743,000 (US$532,608) in dispatch revenue last month, more than double the AU$306,000 generated by the Victorian Big Battery over the same period in Australia.

headline: Battery storage dominates Australia’s Capacity Investment Scheme Tender 7 as hybrid projects secure 7.9GWh
summary_80w: Battery energy storage systems (BESS) have emerged as the defining feature of Australia's Capacity Investment Scheme (CIS) Tender 7, with 2GW/7.9GWh of co-located energy storage successful.

headline: Australia becomes the ‘world’s third-largest utility-scale battery storage market’
summary_80w: Australia has emerged as the world's third-largest utility-scale battery energy storage market, with 4.3GW of large-scale battery storage systems reaching financial close in 2025.

headline: Fortescue starts construction on Western Australia’s ‘largest solar PV power plant’
summary_80w: Mining giant Fortescue has begun construction on the 690MW Turner River solar PV power plant in Western Australia's Pilbara region.

headline: Australia awards 7.8GW of renewable energy under Capacity Investment Scheme Tender 7
summary_80w: Australia's CIS Tender 7 has seen 19 successful projects, which will deliver 7.8GW of renewable energy generation across the NEM.

headline: BloombergNEF ups BESS forecast as renewables add resilience from fossil fuel price shocks
summary_80w: The transition to new energy technologies, including grid-scale and vehicle batteries, can help fossil-fuel-dependent countries improve their energy security.

headline: 250MW/1,000MWh Ontario BESS begins commercial operations, potential expansion ahead
summary_80w: US-headquartered clean energy solutions provider Ameresco’s joint venture (JV) with power producer Atura Power, has begun commercial operations at a 250MW/1,000MWh energy storage project in Ontario, Canada.

headline: US installed 9.7GWh of new BESS in Q1 2026, SEIA reports
summary_80w: The US installed 9.7GWh of new battery energy storage system (BESS) capacity, according to the US trade association group, the Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA).

headline: Contact Energy switches on 200MWh battery storage system in New Zealand
summary_80w: Contact Energy has brought online its first grid-scale BESS, a 100MW/200MWh installation at New Zealand Steel's Glenbrook site.

headline: Masdar picks Sungrow as supplier for world’s largest 24/7 renewable energy project in UAE
summary_80w: Masdar has selected Sungrow for the world’s first gigawatt-scale 24/7 dispatchable renewable energy project in the United Arab Emirates (UAE).

headline: Spearmint Energy secures US$450 million for 600MWh Texas BESS project
summary_80w: Battery storage developer and operator Spearmint Energy has closed a US$450 million financing to support its 300MW/600MWh Red Egret battery energy storage system (BESS) project.

headline: Can Europe become the next major market for Indian solar manufacturers?
summary_80w: As trade dynamics shift, could the EU become the next big market for Indian solar suppliers? PV Tech Premium explores the outlook with Wood Mackenzie’s Yana Hryshko and IEEFA’s Charith Konda.

headline: ‘We’re waiting on robotics for a big paradigm shift’
summary_80w: PV Talk: Frank Oudheusden explains how robotics could create a paradigm shift and improvements in PV system optimisation for extreme weather.

headline: Giant NextEra-Dominion merger should be met with ‘caution’ to avoid rate increases, groups warn
summary_80w: The planned merger of US utilities NextEra Energy and Dominion Energy should be met with “caution” by state lawmakers, according to a number of US clean energy and political non-profit groups.

headline: Polar Racking launches solar O&M services in North America and Caribbean
summary_80w: Polar Racking has launched a Solar Asset Management Division to support operations and maintenance (O&M) activities across utility-scale and commercial solar projects in North America and the Caribbean.

headline: Shorter-term PPAs, sophisticated offtake structures lead discussions at Renewables Procurement & Revenue 2026
summary_80w: On Site Energy's Martin Gaffney said 'We’ve seen PPAs as low as four years,' during this year’s Renewables Procurement & Revenue summit.

headline: Solar PV to dominate ‘electricity-led era’ of the future – BloombergNEF
summary_80w: The world is entering an ‘electricity-led era’, with solar PV set to become the globe’s largest electricity generation technology by 2032, according to Bloomberg New Energy Finance (BloombergNEF).

headline: LDES investor Frontier Power acquires 480MWh Texas BESS portfolio from Bimergen
summary_80w: Long-duration energy storage (LDES) development and investment company Frontier Power USA (FPUSA) has acquired a 480MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) portfolio from developer-operator Bimergen Energy.

headline: Germany market participants starting to price 2029-onwards projects despite uncertainty
summary_80w: The situation and uncertainty around grid connections and grid fees in Germany is evolving, possibly enabling market participants to look beyond the August 2029 grid fee exemption cut-off date - although uncertainty is still very high.

headline: EDF completes 120MWh Poland BESS; Eurus, NGEN and Aretis progress other CEE projects
summary_80w: EDF Power Solutions has commissioned a 50MW/120MWh BESS project in Poland, Eurus has done the same in Hungary, while NGEN and Aretis Group have financed and enlisted optimisers (respectively) for projects in Hungary, Romania, Poland, Slovenia and Latvia.

headline: Developers must become ‘a bit of a firefighter, sound expert, and light expert’: Addressing BESS safety and community opposition
summary_80w: At a recent industry event hosted by market research firm Wood Mackenzie, energy storage experts discussed their approaches to public engagement, especially around addressing fire and other safety concerns.

headline: US ROUNDUP: BESS project advancements from OCI Energy, MN8 Energy, GridStor, Grenergy
summary_80w: In this US news roundup, OCI Energy, MN8 Energy, GridStor, and Grenergy advance battery energy storage system (BESS) projects in Texas, California, Colorado, and Georgia.
